key_insight: LEIA is a real-time learned world model that predicts step-by-step deformation and stress responses of architected materials under interactive loading.
engineering_relevance: It could accelerate solid mechanics design by replacing repeated finite element solves with fast surrogate exploration and ranking of candidate material designs.
method: Autoregressive world model for interactive boundary-condition updates
Supports large 3D unstructured meshes and history-dependent responses
Benchmarked on MicroPlate across explicit lattice and implicit microstructure regimes
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparison against four baselines on the MicroPlate benchmark and validation of stress-accurate candidate ranking against finite element ground truth.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training data covers nonlinear, history-dependent, and multiscale loading regimes, especially for out-of-distribution designs.
paper_title: LEIA: Learned Environment for Interactive Architected Materials
